title: cyanothamnus ramosus subsp. lesueuranus
intro: Cyanothamnus ramosus subsp. lesueuranus, also known as the Tasmanian tea tree, is a plant species native to the island of Tasmania in Australia. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which make it effective against a range of bacterial and fungal infections. The health benefits of Cyanothamnus ramosus subsp. lesueuranus include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Cyanothamnus ramosus subsp. lesueuranus can be prepared as a herbal tea, essential oil, or salve, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
